title: People Analytics
description: Descriptive and inferential statistics applied to decisions in core Human Resources areas, including recruitment and compensation.

title: Data Visualization and Business Analytics
description: Descriptive, predictive, and prescriptive analytics, including forecasting and various statistical techniques.

title: Fundamentals of Financial Accounting
description: Accounting principles, measurements, and business financing forms.

title: Fundamentals of Managerial Accounting
description: Use of accounting data in business decision-making and cost accounting methods.

title: Marketing Management
description: Basic considerations affecting the domestic and international marketing of goods and services.

title: Business Finance
description: Introduction to managerial finance, including financial markets, capital budgeting, and financial planning.

title: Special Topics in Business
description: A specific topics section exploring a focused area of business studies.

title: Managing the Employment Relationship
description: The role of employment relations in enterprise performance; managing the workforce under law and human resource practices.

title: Introduction to Management Information Systems
description: Overview of computer technology and terminology; use of computers as managerial and administrative tools.

title: Management and Organizational Behaviour
description: Behaviour in organizations as it affects people as individuals, their relationships with others, and their performance in groups.

title: Logistics and Operations Management
description: The design and management of systems to make products, provide services and deliver them to the end user.

title: Introduction to Quantitative Decision Making
description: Decision models in business, including basic optimization, linear programming, probability, decision analysis, and spreadsheet applications.

title: Financial Accounting
description: The construction and interpretation of financial reports prepared primarily for external use.

title: Elementary Statistics for Applications
description: Classical, nonparametric, and robust inferences about means, variances, and analysis of variance, using computers. Emphasis on problem formulation, assumptions, and interpretation.

title: Differential Calculus with Applications
description: Derivatives of elementary functions. Applications and modelling: graphing, optimization.

title: Principles of Microeconomics
description: Introduction to consumer behaviour, producer theory, competitive market equilibrium, welfare economics, monopoly, oligopoly, and strategic interactions.

title: Principles of Macroeconomics
description: Theory and Canadian policy regarding growth, business cycles, employment, national income accounting, interest rates, exchange rates, money, banking, and trade.
